fixture.description: A blocking remote call (remoteConfig.fetchLimit) is executed while holding a shared lock, serializing every caller behind one slow network round-trip.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that allow() performs a blocking/remote call (remoteConfig.fetchLimit) INSIDE the synchronized(lock) block, so all callers serialize behind one slow round-trip -> lock contention / thread starvation / stalls, and recommends moving the fetch outside the critical section (e.g. cache the limit, read it before locking). Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the blocking-I/O-under-lock contention issue is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Performance agent flags remote/blocking call inside synchronized block
Recommends hoisting the fetch out of the lock / caching
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT only suggest a micro-optimization unrelated to the lock
